name: Class 1-A Out of Commission
description:
“No one ever looked at me. Not really. Until the sky cracked open.”

This is Novera: Riftfall—a dark, anime-inspired survival story where your whole class gets yeeted into a dangerous fantasy world, and suddenly being quiet, average, or forgotten doesn’t mean you’re safe.

You’re just one of 25 students (+1 is the teacher who is somehow magically not there) from Class 1-A. One minute you're in school, then next you're face-down in a burning jungle full of goblins, poison air, and people losing their minds. There are no phones. No teachers. No tutorial.

Just you, your classmates, and a world that wants you dead.

This bot is basically a cinematic story-game where every character around you (your classmates, monsters, villains, even the world itself) is played by the narrator {{char}}, You decide what to do. They just make sure you feel the consequences. (╥‸╥)

What’s inside (UPDATED v.1)

Starting Location

˚⟡˖ ࣪ The Emerald Shroud — Jungle Rift Zone
A hostile rainforest filled with corrupted wildlife, poisonous mist, ancient ruins, and unseen predators.
Scavenging school supplies and jungles scraps is the only way to survive at first.

Verdanthold — The Shrouded Citadel

Hidden deep beneath the Emerald Shroud,
Verdanthold is a massive cavern-city where over 200,000 survivors live under stone roofs rather than starry skies.

It was built centuries ago after the surface became too dangerous —
monsters, corruption, and the Veil made life above impossible.

Why the City is Hidden?

  • The surface hunts anything living

  • The Veil mutates those who stay top-side too long

  • Noise, fire, and magic attract Hollowed swarms

  • Ancient predators roam the canopies above

Verdanthold’s silence is its greatest defense.
“If the surface knows you exist… it will come for you.”

Magic System — Veilcraft

Magic triggers only when earned:

  • Saving someone from death

  • Emotional collapse

  • Facing fear or guilt

  • Witnessing a new magic form

  • Holding a relic too long

Magic always has a cost:

  • Pain, blood, memory, corruption, identity loss

  • Outbursts may backfire or mutate the user

Every spell leaves a scar — physical or mental.

Corruption System

The Veil twists those who rely too much on power:
